description: SIMD-accelerated crypto verification (Apple M4, ARMv8 Crypto Extensions)
method: criterion benchmark with and without --cfg aes_armv8

aead_encrypt_1kb_chacha20.note: ChaCha20-Poly1305 always uses SIMD — no cfg flag needed

aead_encrypt_1kb_aes256gcm_software.note: Without aes_armv8 cfg flag — software AES (constant-time)

aead_decrypt_1kb_aes256gcm_software.note: Without aes_armv8 cfg flag — software AES

aead_encrypt_1kb_aes256gcm_hardware.note: With aes_armv8 cfg flag — hardware ARMv8 AES instructions

aead_encrypt_64b_aes256gcm_hardware.note: Small message — AES-256-GCM with hardware AES

key_findings.aes256gcm_vs_chacha20_1kb: AES-256-GCM with hardware AES is 1.5x faster than ChaCha20-Poly1305 for 1KB (1.09µs vs 1.64µs)
key_findings.software_aes_is_slow: Without aes_armv8 cfg flag, AES-256-GCM is 3.1x SLOWER than ChaCha20-Poly1305 (5.08µs vs 1.64µs)
key_findings.target_assessment: The <0.3µs target for 1KB AEAD is not achievable — actual floor is 1.09µs with hardware AES. For 64B messages, both algorithms are ~230ns (under 300ns).
key_findings.protocol_impact: AAFP uses ChaCha20-Poly1305 by default. Switching to AES-256-GCM would give 1.5x speedup for 1KB+ messages but would be a wire protocol change — NOT recommended.

configuration_change.rationale: All Apple Silicon (M1-M4) has ARMv8 AES instructions. This enables hardware AES for the aes-gcm crate.
